item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to talk to Roget about his headcanon regarding this article, and it was stated that a crosslink between here and SCP-1387 would be a nice idea. Looking into how such a connection could be made, perhaps instances of SCP-1387 will occasionally land at our little pocket airport and nab some of the sitting duck passengers stuck there. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

Subjects who exit at the layover will enter a location which appears to be an exact replica of the Jacksonville International Airport terminal, which is fully staffed. All electrical outlets are functional , but communication equipment such as cellular phones and internet-capable devices will not charge. They will, however, function and receive signal until their power is depleted. Phone records will show the calls originated from the Hollywood International Airport. No planes have been observed approaching the departure terminals, and planes approaching the entrance terminal have originated exclusively from the Hollywood International Airport.

To this:

Subjects who exit at the layover will enter a location which appears to be an exact replica of the Jacksonville International Airport terminal, which is fully staffed. All electrical outlets are functional , but communication equipment such as cellular phones and internet-capable devices will not charge. They will, however, function and receive signal until their power is depleted. Phone records will show the calls originated from the Hollywood International Airport. No planes have been observed approaching the departure terminals1, and planes approaching the entrance terminal have originated exclusively from the Hollywood International Airport.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. It has been observed on several occasions for instances of SCP-1387 to arrive at departure terminals to intiaite their anomalous effects on subjects stuck within SCP-1494. How instances of SCP-1387 are able to access SCP-1494 is currently under investigation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only in the sense that it takes place in a bizarro-pocket-universe airport

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh…

So is it like the Langoliers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cell phones of passengers still function. Sorry for necroposting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, probably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get the feeling that i want to know more, but if i did it would be underwhelming :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So much what. I like it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subjects will believe the accusations are legitimate, whether or not they have committed any type of treason, or if the action they are accused of is even possible.

Accused of "high treason against the crown" and told that returning to their home or to any other state would cause them to be extradited and executed "on the King's pleasure." The document bearing this information bore the royal mark of King Charles X of France.

So does that subject now believe that France is currently ruled by a king? Or does she simultaneously believe that France is democracy and that she committed high treason against the crown?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this. Most of the piece seemed to hold together OK, but then that addendum was just a total WTF? out of left field. Like Ishmael, I get the feeling I'm Missing Something here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read the bugging/tracking as more aimed at research than containment.
Transferring 45 year old copilots and restricting coke is to prevent the "layover" from happening, planting bugs is to find out what goes on when it does happen.

Of course, if my reading is right, the bugs really shouldn't be mentioned in the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By and large, i like this, but that last addendum confuses me. It seems like it's hinting at whatever caused the anomaly, but i can't piece together anyhting out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool, this is interesting, I can dig it… but how did we find this, exactly? Moreso, how did we get so much information on this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or not serve coke on flights leaving that airport.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One quibble. If there are specific conditions in which the anomalous event manifests, why go through the trouble of bugging and tracking every flight?

EDIT: Just reread it, and it says that the conditions won't manidest if there are Foundation personnel on board. Wouldn't it be easier to just have an Agent attend each flight that left the airport, rather than waste resources bugging every flight?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehh…I can tell you put some work into this, but the payoff felt kind of weak. And this is esoteric on the "somewhat frustrating" side rather than the "really intriguing" side".

So, neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All flights exiting the Hollywood International Airport located in Ft. Lauderdale, FL, are to be fitted with surveillance and tracking devices, in the luggage and on random passengers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, so how does the Foundation know what happens at the layover?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a backstory but it's not really anything mind-blowing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. Interesting, unique, and quite creepy. I will say that the addendum could use some clarity… it's a little confusing as written.

Is there supposed to be some underlying, hidden thing going on here beyond what is written? I didn't catch it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dang it, I was writing a draft that was similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted very early into my reading, and was not disappointed. I don't even quite know what is supposed to be going on exactly, but I loved it anyway. I loved the visual of the communes being built out of luggage, and people scavenging for supplies from new luggage being brought into the airport.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I know what's going on here. I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something wonderful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a lot better now the 'treason' stuff is in it. I eat the suspicious words I said before about that. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um, what the fuck am I reading?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know we have at least one SCP on the theme of airline flights where anomalous things happen in midair, but I don't think any of them have focused on the alienation in the boarding/disembarking process, and this one does a decent job of alluding to that. Nice implementation of my idea, too.

One small nitpick: when I suggested that the conditions would read better phrased differently, I meant that they should all be reworded that way, not just the example I used. Now they look even more inconsistent.
